scope_boundary: synthetic claim-ledger and belief-revision record discipline only; no source interpretation, open-domain claim extraction, contradiction detection, verifier-quality result, deployed belief engine, semantic-equivalence check, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has claim-record and belief-revision schemas, protocol fixtures, finite Claim Ledger Lean predicates, a proof adequacy review that keeps the target useful but too narrow, exact source mappings for the Claim Ledgers chapter, and a synthetic claim-ledger revision harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make no-change revision records, contradiction quarantine, claim splitting, history preservation, affected-surface propagation, review routing, residuals, and support-state promotion blockers executable at the record level. They still do not establish source interpretation adequacy, open-domain claim extraction, contradiction detection quality, semantic equivalence across rewritten claims, verifier quality, or a deployed belief-revision engine.
required_artifacts: claim extraction run against a known corpus with expected claim identities
contradiction detection benchmark or reviewed fixture suite with positive and negative controls
surface-synchronization test showing chapter, appendix, reader, release, and proof surfaces update after a claim revision
semantic-equivalence review for split, merged, narrowed, or deprecated claims
verifier integration trace showing proof, citation, procedure, or tribunal results feeding the ledger
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_claim_ledger_revision.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: schema validation checks record shape rather than belief-engine correctness
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for semantic claim-revision adequacy
claim extraction, contradiction detection, semantic-equivalence review, surface synchronization, and verifier integration are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as open-domain claim extraction evidence
contradiction quarantine fixture interpreted as contradiction-detection quality evidence
belief-revision record validity interpreted as a deployed belief engine
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after claim extraction, contradiction detection, surface synchronization, semantic-equivalence review, verifier integration, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not extract claims from source text
synthetic contradiction quarantine does not measure contradiction-detection quality
claim splitting fixtures do not prove semantic equivalence or reviewer agreement
surface refs in fixtures do not prove generated book, reader, release, and proof surfaces synchronize after real revisions
finite Lean predicates do not implement a belief-revision engine
no independent epistemic-systems or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent epistemic-systems, formal-methods, verifier-quality, or source-interpretation audit
non_claims: does not promote Claim Ledgers above argument
does not prove open-domain claim extraction
does not prove contradiction detection quality
does not prove deployed belief revision or runtime behavior
